title: Utada Hikaru 30 theme song Sakura Nagashi
display_title: Utada Hikaru 30 theme song Sakura Nagashi
wikitext:
[]: Utada Hikaru 3.0 theme song Sakura Nagashi             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Nov 16, 2012 10:57 am http://room666.blog49.fc2.com/blog-entry-6372.html 桜流し Sakura nagashi

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:00 am So she came out of Partying...I mean hiatus ? Anyway, the song sounds great.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaia » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:02 am oh wow dude beat me to it haha... I pretty much signed up just to post that haha ;___; EDIT: deleted the thread I started

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:05 am Zaia wrote: oh wow dude beat me to it

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:05 am Woof, that kicks in something fierce with the strings and all :)

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:07 am That was pretty great. It makes me speculate what kind of note the movie ends on to have such a moody song.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:08 am There's gonna be quite a lot of bawling in the theaters when the movie ends with this song. The first showing ends in about 40 minutes!

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:17 am huh, that was unexpectedly decent. Songwriting's nothing special but Utada's vocals really carry the song well. Nice. I can imagine it fitting 3.0 credits very well

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:24 am I found it boring. Note: This is true of almost all music I have ever listened to on its own. One consistent pattern I have noticed throughout my life is that I only like music once it's been associated with other experiences. E.g., I didn't care one whit for Ode to Joy till Evangelion. So how this song is used in the movie, if at all, will likely change my opinion.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:27 am ^ Let's voice our unpopular opinion in this case, it really didn't say much to me either. Personally I already cared for Ode to Joy and other classical pieces used in Eva but effectively it was because I already associated them to something since I was a kid (for example Ode to Joy is the European Hymn).

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:33 am The entire continent has a hymn? Are you talking about the EU?

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:35 am A.T. Fish wrote: The entire continent has a hymn, or are you talking about the EU?

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:36 am It was good as far as J-Pop songs go, but I prefer Cruel Angel's Thesis and Komm Susser Tod (I'm still hoping one or both of those will show up in 4.0). Then again, if it's used well i could come to appreciate it more.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:37 am Shinoyami65 wrote: It was good as far as J-Pop songs go, but I prefer Cruel Angel's Thesis and Komm Susser Tod (I'm still hoping one or both of those will show up in 4.0). Then again, if it's used well i could come to appreciate it more.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:41 am I don't care much for Japanese music, but the latter half of the song was intense enough that I liked it.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:42 am riffraff11235 wrote: we might only hear small clips of Komm Susser Todd .

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:44 am Monk Ed wrote: I found it boring. Note: This is true of almost all music I have ever listened to on its own. One consistent pattern I have noticed throughout my life is that I only like music once it's been associated with other experiences. E.g., I didn't care one whit for Ode to Joy till Evangelion. So how this song is used in the movie, if at all, will likely change my opinion.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:49 am Newspost made: http://www.evageeks.org/2012/11/utada-hikaru-new-3-0-end-theme/

[]:             Quote Post by Amitch1991 » Fri Nov 16, 2012 2:01 pm I think the song is absolutely amazing, such a beautiful video to go with it, although it seems to me like it is sung in a dark way ( i really can't speak much Japanese other than a few basic phrases), so it seems to me like something happens in the film to warrant such a "dark" closing song. (Mere Speculation at this point of course ;) )

[]:             Quote Post by Knuckles » Fri Nov 16, 2012 2:02 pm "Everybody fights... in the end." Oh my god, I'm almost tearing up just THINKING about what this song is going to play after. I'm SO scared to watch 3.0 now....
